556	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
557	
558	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
559	a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
560	neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
561	iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
562	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
563	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
564	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
565	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/
566	
567	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
568	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
569	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
570	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
571	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
572	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
573	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
574	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
575	    IsIso φ := by
576	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
577	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
578	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
579	    intro x
580	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
581	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
582	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
583	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
584	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
585	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
586	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
587	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
588	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
589	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
590	    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
591	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
592	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
593	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
594	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
595	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
596	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
597	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
598	  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
599	  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
600	  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
601	  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by
602	    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
603	    exact this
604	  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1
605	
606	/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `𝒪_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module
607	morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
608	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
609	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
610	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
611	(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
612	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
613	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
614	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
615	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
616	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
617	    M ⟶ N :=
618	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩
619	
620	/-- The underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `homMk g hg` is `g` itself. -/
621	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map_homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
622	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
623	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
624	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
625	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=
626	  rfl

775	    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
776	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
777	  ⟨(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
778	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
779	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
780	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
781	    tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩
782	
783	/-- The setoid of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules: `M ∼ M'` iff there exists an
784	isomorphism `M ≅ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
785	instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
786	  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) ≅ (M' : X.Modules))
787	  iseqv :=
788	    ⟨fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ⟨f⟩ => ⟨e ≪≫ f⟩⟩
789	
790	/-- **Step 2 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group** (blueprint
791	`def:pic_carrier`): the quotient of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules by isomorphism. -/
792	def PicGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := Quotient (picSetoid X)
793	
794	/-- Multiplication on `PicGroup X`: `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, well-defined on
795	iso-classes by bifunctoriality (`tensorObjIsoOfIso`), landing in `PicGroup` by
796	`IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
797	noncomputable def picMul {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
798	  Quotient.lift₂
799	    (fun a b => Quotient.mk _ ⟨tensorObj a.1 b.1, a.2.tensorObj b.2⟩)
800	    (by
801	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨b, hb⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨b', hb'⟩ ⟨ea⟩ ⟨eb⟩
802	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObjIsoOfIso ea eb⟩)
803	
804	/-- The inverse class on `PicGroup X`: `[M] ↦ [N]` for the membership witness `N`
805	of `IsInvertible M`, well-defined by `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`. -/
806	noncomputable def picInv {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
807	  Quotient.lift
808	    (fun a => Quotient.mk _
809	      ⟨Classical.choose a.2,
810	        a.1, ⟨tensorObj_braiding _ a.1 ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).some⟩⟩)
811	    (by
812	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨ea⟩
813	      refine Quotient.sound ?_
814	      -- both `Classical.choose ha` and `Classical.choose ha'` are tensor inverses of `a`
815	      have h1 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
816	        (Classical.choose_spec ha).some
817	      have h2 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha') ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
818	        tensorObjIsoOfIso ea (Iso.refl _) ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec ha').some
819	      exact IsInvertible.inverse_unique h1 h2)
820	
821	/-- **Step 6 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group is abelian** (blueprint
822	`thm:pic_commgroup`). `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, `1 := [𝒪_X]`, and `[M]⁻¹` the
823	class of any membership witness of `IsInvertible M`. Each group axiom is a single
824	existence-of-isomorphism: unit laws ← unitors, associativity ← associator,
825	commutativity ← braiding, left inverse ← the witness iso. No monoidal coherence. -/
826	noncomputable instance picCommGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : CommGroup (PicGroup X) where
827	  mul := picMul
828	  one := Quotient.mk _ ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, isInvertible_unit⟩
829	  inv := picInv
830	  mul_assoc := by
831	    rintro a b c
832	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
833	    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
834	    induction c using Quotient.ind with | _ c => ?_
835	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_assoc_iso⟩
836	  one_mul := by
837	    rintro a
838	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
839	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_left_unitor a.1⟩
840	  mul_one := by
841	    rintro a
842	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
843	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_right_unitor a.1⟩
844	  inv_mul_cancel := by
845	    rintro a
846	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
847	    exact Quotient.sound
848	      ⟨tensorObj_braiding (Classical.choose a.2) a.1 ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).some⟩
849	  mul_comm := by
850	    rintro a b
851	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
852	    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
853	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_braiding a.1 b.1⟩

855	/-! ## §6. Pullback-monoidality substrate (A.1.c): `IsInvertible.pullback`
856	
857	Project-local Mathlib supplement. The relative Picard consumer re-bases onto the
858	`IsInvertible` carrier and its structure maps are *pullback* maps for GENERAL scheme
859	morphisms (the projection `C ×_S T → T` and base-change maps are neither open
860	immersions nor flat). We need that pullback preserves `⊗`-invertibility. This requires
861	`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`
862	(`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`). Blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`. -/
863	
864	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit→pushforward-unit comparison.**
865	
866	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
867	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons
868	of `f` and `h` and the pushforward pseudofunctor coherence `pushforwardComp`. This is
869	the *pushforward-side* (right-adjoint) half of the composition coherence; it is
870	concrete (sectionwise it is just functoriality of the structure-sheaf ring maps,
871	hence `rfl` after the `ext`-chain) and is the pushforward-side input from which the
872	genuinely-needed *pullback-side* coherence of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is obtained by
873	adjunction-mate transport. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit. -/
874	lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
875	    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
876	      SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
877	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
878	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
879	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf) := by
880	  apply SheafOfModules.Hom.ext
881	  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
882	  intro U
883	  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
884	  apply LinearMap.ext
885	  intro a
886	  rfl
887	
888	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
889	(the genuinely-new ingredient for `pullbackUnitIso`).**
890	
891	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
892	`f^*𝒪 ⟶ 𝒪` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons of `f` and `h`
893	and the pullback pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackComp`:
894	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu h`.
895	
896	This is the pullback-side (left-adjoint) composition coherence — Mathlib-absent at the
897	pinned commit and NOT a sectionwise statement (the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no
898	sectionwise value). It is obtained by adjunction-mate transport from the pushforward-side
899	coherence `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`: transposing both sides under
900	`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)`, the left side becomes `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h≫f)`
901	and the right side is reassembled via the conjugate/mate identity
902	`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (relating `pullbackComp.inv` to `pushforwardComp.hom`),
903	`unit_conjugateEquiv`, and the composite-adjunction unit `Adjunction.comp_unit_app`.
904	
905	Consumed by `pullbackUnitIso`: on an affine chart `V` the inclusion `V.ι ≫ f` factors as
906	`g ≫ U.ι` with `Opens.map g.base` `Final`, so two applications of this coherence (one for
907	each factorisation) express the restricted global comparison as a composite of isomorphisms
908	(`pbu` for an open immersion / a `Final`-chart morphism is an iso), whence
909	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an iso by `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`.
910	
911	The proof uses `erw` for the associativity / functoriality steps because the `SheafOfModules`
912	category compositions appear in defeq-but-not-syntactic forms (`Scheme.Modules.pullback f`
913	vs `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`) on which plain `rw [Category.assoc]` /
914	`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fail to unify. -/
915	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
916	    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
917	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
918	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
919	      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
920	  have key := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp h f
921	  have conj := unit_conjugateEquiv
922	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
923	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h))
924	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f))
925	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
926	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at conj
927	  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
928	  have hL : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
929	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom)
930	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom := by
931	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
932	      (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom
933	  have hi : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
934	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
935	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
936	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
937	          (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
938	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app
939	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj
940	            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := by
941	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]; exact conj.symm
942	  have hLf : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _
943	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
944	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom := by
945	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
946	      f.toRingCatSheafHom
947	  have hLh : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
948	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
949	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
950	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
951	      h.toRingCatSheafHom
952	  have hinner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).unit.app
953	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) ≫
954	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h).map
955	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
956	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
957	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
958	    = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
959	        SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
960	    have e := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)
961	      (X := (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
962	      (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf)
963	      (f := (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
964	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
965	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
966	    have key2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
967	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
968	              (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
969	            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
970	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
971	            SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
972	      rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left]; exact congrArg _ hLh
973	    exact e.symm.trans key2
974	  have hcomp' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
975	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
976	        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
977	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
978	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
979	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
980	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
981	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
982	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
983	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) := by
984	    have ef := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
985	      (X := SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)
986	      (f := SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
987	    rw [Adjunction.comp_unit_app, Functor.comp_map]
988	    erw [Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, hinner, Functor.map_comp]
989	    erw [← Category.assoc]

991	            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
992	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map

996	  rw [hL, key, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, hi]
997	  erw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.naturality
998	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫

1003	/-! ### Phase 1 — `pullbackUnitIso` (`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`), blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`
1004	
1005	**iter-241 RESOLUTION (the chart-chase is NOT needed).** The unit comparison
1006	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for *every* morphism of
1007	schemes `f`, not just for `Final`-chart morphisms. The Mathlib instance
1008	`SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` fires whenever the comparison
1009	functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`, and that functor is **always** `Final`: the
1010	preimage functor on opens preserves finite limits (it is a frame homomorphism), so it is
1011	representably flat, whence `final_of_representablyFlat` supplies `(Opens.map f.base).Final`
1012	unconditionally. (Verified axiom-clean for a general `f`.) The elaborate affine
1013	chart-chase contemplated by the blueprint proof — and the iter-240 coherence linchpin
1014	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` — are therefore unnecessary for this lemma (the linchpin is
1015	retained above as it is the genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence, of independent
1016	use for the harder Phase-2 tensor comparison).
1017	
1018	The remaining friction was purely a Lean typeclass-resolution accident: in a context with
1019	several `(Opens.map _).Final` hypotheses (or after a `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite)
1020	the buried implicit instance args of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (`[F.IsContinuous]`,
1021	`[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`) are defeq-but-not-syntactic, so `asIso`/`infer_instance`
1022	fails to synthesise `IsIso (pbu f)`. The fix (mathlib-analogist `pbu-canon`, the
1023	`Functor.Monoidal.μIso` idiom): isolate the single `Final` hypothesis in the helper
1024	`isIso_pbu_of_final` whose body `inferInstance` runs at a clean site, then transport the
1025	resulting witness through `asIso` by passing it *explicitly* (`@asIso … (isIso_pbu_of_final g)`)
1026	— the application's defeq check runs at default transparency and succeeds, whereas instance
1027	synthesis (reducible transparency) does not. -/
1028	
1029	/-- **`IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit g)` from a single `Final` hypothesis, at a clean site.**
1030	Project-local: isolates the lone `(Opens.map g.base).Final` instance so that the Mathlib
1031	`OfFinal` instance synthesises without colliding with other in-scope `Final`/`IsIso`
1032	hypotheses (see the section note). -/
1033	private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1034	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1035	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
1036	
1037	/-- **Bundled `Iso` form of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g`** for a `Final`
1038	comparison functor — the analogue of `CategoryTheory.Functor.Monoidal.μIso`. Project-local:
1039	hands out the isomorphism (rather than a bare `IsIso` instance) so downstream coherence
1040	reasoning stays at the `Iso` level and never re-triggers the `pbu` instance synthesis. -/
1041	noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1042	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1043	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1044	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
1045	  @asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
1046	    (isIso_pbu_of_final g)
1047	
1048	@[simp] lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1049	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1050	    (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g).hom =
1051	      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom := rfl
1052	
1053	/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf** (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`):
1054	`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` for an arbitrary morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X`, where
1055	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`. The comparison functor `Opens.map f.base` is
1056	always `Final` (preimage on opens is representably flat), so the Mathlib unit comparison
1057	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism unconditionally. -/
1058	noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
1059	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1060	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
1061	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
1062	  pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f

1064	/-- **Sheafification reconciles the presheaf tensor with the tensor of the
1065	sheafified factors.** For presheaves of modules `P, Q` on `X`, sheafifying the
1066	presheaf tensor `P ⊗ Q` agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying
1067	presheaves of their sheafifications `(a P).val ⊗ (a Q).val`, where
1068	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 𝒪_X)`. This is the "sheafification is
1069	monoidal" reconciliation, built — exactly as in `tensorObj_assoc_iso` — by
1070	whiskering the sheafification unit `η` (a `J.W`-morphism, hence locally bijective)
1071	on each side and inverting under `a` via `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` together
1072	with the flatness-free `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`. It is the bridge reconciling a
1073	presheaf-level tensorator with the substrate `⊗_X` (whose `.val` carries an extra
1074	sheafification on each factor), as needed by the pullback-monoidality comparison
1075	`pullbackTensorIso`. -/
1076	private noncomputable def sheafifyTensorUnitIso {X : Scheme.{u}}
1077	    (P Q : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1078	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1079	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) P Q) ≅
1080	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1081	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)
1082	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
1083	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P).val
1084	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
1085	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj Q).val) := by
1086	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=

1088	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1089	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha

1091	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
1092	  have hηP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1093	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P)) := by
1094	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
1095	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
1096	  have hηQ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1097	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app Q)) := by

1112	/-- **The presheaf-of-modules pushforward is lax monoidal** (the analogist-named `μ_G`,
1113	Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `φ : S₀ ⋙ forget₂ ⟶ F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`

1120	The hypothesis necessarily uses the *inner* `forget₂` association (`F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`,

1126	to `pushforward φ` — is transported back by `exact`. This is the right-adjoint lax structure

1132	    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1133	      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1134	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal := by
1135	  let φ' : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1136	      (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := φ
1137	  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙
1138	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ').LaxMonoidal := inferInstance
1139	  exact h

1236	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1: the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition
1237	
1238	These are general `PresheafOfModules` statements (any `F : C ⥤ D`, ring presheaves
1239	`R, S`), not specific to schemes; they are project-local because Mathlib's pinned commit
1240	exposes neither `extendScalars` nor `pullback₀` at the presheaf-of-modules level. Both are
1241	realised as the left adjoint of an existing right adjoint: `pushforward₀ F R` is
1242	definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))` (because `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose,
1243	witnessed by Mathlib's `restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full`), and
1244	`restrictScalars φ` is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`; since `pushforward _` is
1245	always a right adjoint, so are these two, and their left adjoints `pullback₀`/`extendScalars`
1246	exist. The decomposition `pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀` then follows from the
1247	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ` by
1248	uniqueness of left adjoints (`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso`).
1249	
1250	Per blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition` (D1). **OFF-PATH (iter-243 pivot).** D1 is
1251	axiom-clean and self-contained, but it was the first brick of the ABANDONED general
1252	strong-monoidal pullback build (`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, route (e)); the active
1253	route is the loc-triv chart-chase D1'–D4' (§below), which does NOT consume `extendScalars`/
1254	`pullback₀`. Retained as a correct, reusable presheaf-level decomposition; do NOT extend it
1255	toward the general build (D2 scalar-strong / D3 topological-interchange are NOT pursued). -/
1256	
1257	section PullbackLanDecomposition
1258	
1259	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1260	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
1261	
1262	/-- `pushforward₀ F R` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))`
1263	(since `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose). Project-local; carries the existence of the
1264	topological inverse image `pullback₀`. -/
1265	private lemma pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1266	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀.{u} F R).IsRightAdjoint :=
1267	  inferInstanceAs (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))).IsRightAdjoint
1268	
1269	/-- `restrictScalars φ` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`.
1270	Project-local; carries the existence of the extension of scalars `extendScalars`. -/
1271	private lemma restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1272	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
1273	  inferInstanceAs
1274	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (F := 𝟭 C) (R := F.op ⋙ R) φ).IsRightAdjoint
1275	
1276	/-- **The topological inverse image `pullback₀ := (pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint`**, the
1277	left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward. On underlying presheaves it is the left Kan
1278	extension along `F.op`. Project-local (no `PresheafOfModules` inverse-image functor at the
1279	pin); the carrier of D3. -/
1280	noncomputable def pullback0 (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1281	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R :=
1282	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1283	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint
1284	
1285	/-- **Extension of scalars `extendScalars φ := (restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint`**, the left
1286	adjoint of restriction of scalars along a morphism of ring presheaves. Project-local; the
1287	carrier of D2 (strong monoidal via `distribBaseChange`). -/
1288	noncomputable def extendScalars (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1289	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} S ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) :=
1290	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1291	  (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint
1292	
1293	/-- The adjunction `pullback₀ ⊣ pushforward₀`. -/
1294	noncomputable def pullback0Adjunction (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1295	    pullback0 F R ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R :=
1296	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1297	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R)
1298	
1299	/-- The adjunction `extendScalars φ ⊣ restrictScalars φ`. -/
1300	noncomputable def extendScalarsAdjunction (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1301	    extendScalars φ ⊣ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ :=
1302	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1303	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ)
1304	
1305	/-- **D1 — the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition** (blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).
1306	For `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules, the presheaf
1307	pullback factors as extension of scalars followed by the topological inverse image,
1308	`pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀`. This is the left-adjoint reversal of the
1309	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, obtained
1310	from `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (uniqueness of left adjoints). Project-local. -/
1311	noncomputable def pullbackLanDecomposition (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1312	    PresheafOfModules.pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback0 F R :=
1313	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
1314	    (extendScalarsAdjunction φ) (pullback0Adjunction F R)
1315	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
1316	    (Iso.refl (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ))).symm
1317	
1318	end PullbackLanDecomposition

1355	lemma isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta {X Y : Scheme.{u}}

1358	          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=

1361	          (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1362	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val))) :
1363	    IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N) := by

1367	  haveI hδ : IsIso (a_Y.map
1368	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val)) := h
1369	  -- pieces 1 and 3 are `Iso.hom`s; piece 4 is `a_Y.map` of the (iso) `⊗ₘ` of the two

1371	  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (IsIso.comp_isIso' hδ
1372	    (IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso f M N)))

1380	iso (the D2' η-bridge route, below). -/
1381	private lemma W_of_isIso_sheafification {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
1382	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
1383	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
1384	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
1385	    {A B : _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)

1388	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
1389	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
1390	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := hf
1391	  rw [← h] at h2

67	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
68	
69	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
70	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
71	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/

73	namespace PresheafOfModules
74	
75	open InternalHom Opposite
76	
77	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
78	
79	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
80	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
81	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
82	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
83	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
84	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
85	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
86	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
87	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
89	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
90	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
91	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
92	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
93	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
94	  exact
95	    { toFun := fun φ =>
96	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
97	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
98	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
99	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
100	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
101	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
102	      invFun := fun r =>
103	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
104	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
105	      left_inv := fun φ => by
106	        ext Y
107	        dsimp only
108	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
109	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
110	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
111	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
112	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
113	        rfl
114	      right_inv := fun r => by
115	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
116	            (restr X.unop
117	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
118	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
119	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
120	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
121	        exact mul_one r }
122	
123	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
124	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
125	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
126	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
127	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
128	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
129	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
130	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
131	    (fun {X Y} f => by
132	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
133	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
134	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
135	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
136	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
137	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
138	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
139	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
140	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
141	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
142	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
143	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
144	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
145	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
146	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
147	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
148	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
149	      rw [hrm] at key
150	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
151	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
152	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
153	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
154	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
155	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
156	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
157	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
158	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
159	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
160	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
161	
162	end PresheafOfModules

170	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
171	
172	open Opposite in
173	/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion
174	structure ring iso `(f.appIso W').inv` is an isomorphism.**  Its underlying map is the (bijective)
175	ring map `(f.appIso W').inv.hom`, so `ε` is an iso by `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`
176	(`PresheafInternalHom.lean`) fed the bijectivity from `ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`.  This
177	is the single load-bearing fact powering `dualUnitRingSwap` (the codomain unit ring swap of leg-B),
178	phrased at the `CommRingCat` carrier so `CommRing` is native (per `analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
179	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
180	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
181	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
182	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)) :=
183	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom
184	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv)
185	
186	open Opposite in
187	/-- **Leg-B: the codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW')) ⟶
188	𝟙_Y(W')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)`,
189	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`.  The endpoints are written at the canonical
190	`CommRingCat` section carriers `↑(X.presheaf.obj _)` / `↑(Y.presheaf.obj _)` (the `forget₂`-composite
191	carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis, `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); they reconcile by
192	`rfl`/defeq with the `restr`/`𝟙_`-section spellings of `sliceDualTransport`'s `codomainMap` hole. -/
193	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
194	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
195	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
196	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) ⟶
197	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W'))) :=
198	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
199	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
200	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))
201	
202	open Opposite in
203	/-- **Leg-B (inverse direction): the unit codomain ring-iso swap for `invFun`** `𝟙_Y(W') ⟶
204	restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW'))`.  This is the lax-monoidal unit
205	`ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)` ITSELF (not its inverse), the reverse of
206	`dualUnitRingSwap`.  By `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso` it is an isomorphism and is the inverse of
207	`dualUnitRingSwap f W'` (they cancel by `IsIso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id`). -/
208	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwapInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
209	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
210	    (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))) ⟶
211	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
212	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) :=
213	  Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)
214	
215	open Opposite in
216	/-- `dualUnitRingSwapInv` is a section of `dualUnitRingSwap` (`ε ≫ inv ε = 𝟙`). -/
217	@[simp] lemma dualUnitRingSwapInv_comp_dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
218	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
219	    dualUnitRingSwapInv f W' ≫ dualUnitRingSwap f W' = 𝟙 _ := by
220	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
221	  simp [dualUnitRingSwapInv, dualUnitRingSwap]
222	
223	open Opposite in
224	/-- `dualUnitRingSwap` is a section of `dualUnitRingSwapInv` (`inv ε ≫ ε = 𝟙`). -/
225	@[simp] lemma dualUnitRingSwap_comp_dualUnitRingSwapInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
226	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
227	    dualUnitRingSwap f W' ≫ dualUnitRingSwapInv f W' = 𝟙 _ := by
228	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
229	  simp [dualUnitRingSwapInv, dualUnitRingSwap]

231	open Opposite in
232	/-- **`invFun` codomain ε is an iso (`.hom` direction).**  The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of
233	`restrictScalars` along `(f.appIso W').hom` (the `.hom`, not `.inv`, of the structure ring iso) is
234	an isomorphism, since `(f.appIso W').hom` is a bijective ring map.  This powers the `invFun`
235	codomain swap (which reindexes the `Over V` section back across `f.opensFunctor` using the
236	`.hom` direction, the mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap`'s `.inv`). -/
237	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
238	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
239	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
240	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom)) :=
241	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom
242	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom)
243	
244	open Opposite in
245	/-- **`invFun` codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').hom (𝟙_Y(W')) ⟶
246	𝟙_X(fW')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').hom)`,
247	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom`.  This is the codomain swap of the reverse
248	transport `invFun` (mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap`, using the `.hom` direction). -/
249	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwapHom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
250	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
251	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom).obj
252	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))) ⟶
253	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W')))) :=
254	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom f W'
255	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
256	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom))

258	open PresheafOfModules InternalHom Opposite in
259	/-- **Leg (A)∘(B): the sectionwise slice transport of the dual along an open immersion.**
260	
261	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X`, `M : X.Modules`, and an open `V` of `Y` (as `(Opens Y)ᵒᵖ`),
262	this is the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear isomorphism between the two sectionwise values of the Step-4 residual
263	of `dual_restrict_iso`:
264	```
265	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V  ≅  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
266	```
267	where `β` is the open-immersion structure ring morphism `Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙
268	X.ringCatSheaf` (`β.app U = (forget₂ _ _).map (f.appIso U).inv`).
269	
270	The construction mirrors `homLocalSection` (the thin-poset `eqToHom`-conjugation slice transport)
271	composed with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear codomain-unit ring swap of leg
272	(B)): a dual section `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over (fV)` is reindexed across
273	`f.opensFunctor` to a dual section over `Over V`, conjugating each component by the structure ring
274	iso `f.appIso`; naturality on the thin poset `Opens Y` is `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
275	noncomputable def sliceDualTransport {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
276	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ) :
277	    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
278	      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
279	    letI β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
280	      Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
281	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)).obj V) ≅
282	      ((PresheafOfModules.dual ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V) := by
283	  -- CONSTRUCTION PLAN (homLocalSection-style leg (A) ∘ restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv leg (B)):
284	  --
285	  -- Write `fV := f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`.  By `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_obj`,
286	  --   LHS carrier `L = (dual M.val).obj (op fV) = (restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X)`,
287	  --     a `𝒪_X(fV)`-module restricted along `β.app V : 𝒪_Y(V) ⟶ 𝒪_X(fV)` to a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module;
288	  --   RHS carrier `Rr = (restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val) ⟶ restr V.unop 𝟙_Y)`,
289	  --     a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module via `internalHomObjModule`.
290	  --
291	  -- Build a `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear equivalence `e : L ≃ₗ[𝒪_Y(V)] Rr` and return `e.toModuleIso`.
292	  --
293	  -- `e.toFun φ` (for `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X`) is the dual section over `Over V`
294	  -- whose component at `W : (Over V.unop)ᵒᵖ` (so `W' := W.unop.left ≤ V.unop`, with image
295	  -- `fW' := f.opensFunctor.obj W'`) is
296	  --   `(restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj W  ≃defeq  M.val.obj (op fW')`
297	  --     --[ φ.app (op (Over.mk (f.opensFunctor.map W.unop.hom))) ]-->  X.ring(fW')
298	  --     --[ (f.appIso W').hom : 𝒪_X(fW') ≅ 𝒪_Y(W') ]-->  Y.ring(W')  =  (restr V.unop 𝟙_Y).obj W,
299	  -- packaged as a `ModuleCat` hom over `𝒪_Y(W')`.  Naturality of this family in `W` is automatic
300	  -- on the thin poset `Opens Y` (`Subsingleton.elim` on the base maps, exactly as in
301	  -- `homLocalSection`'s `naturality` field).  `e.invFun` is the same with `(f.appIso W').inv` and
302	  -- the inverse reindexing (every `W'' ≤ fV` is `f.opensFunctor.obj (f⁻¹ᵁ W'')` since
303	  -- `fV ⊆ range f`); `left_inv`/`right_inv` collapse by `Iso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id` of `f.appIso`
304	  -- plus the down-set bijection `image_preimage_of_le`.  `𝒪_Y(V)`-linearity (`map_smul'`) is the
305	  -- `globalSMul`/`homModule`-action compatibility (post-composition with the structure scalar),
306	  -- intertwined by the ring iso — the presheaf-level shadow of `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`'s
307	  -- `map_smul'`.
308	  --
309	  -- The single load-bearing sub-build is `e.toFun`'s underlying `PresheafOfModules.Hom`; it is a
310	  -- structural copy of `homLocalSection` (component conjugation by `eqToHom` + the `f.appIso` ring
311	  -- iso) and of `dualPrecompEquiv` (the `≃ₗ` packaging).
312	  --

354	  letI lhsMod : Module (Y.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V : Type u)
355	      (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)).obj V : Type u) :=

367	            -- carrier-instance loss that raw `ModuleCat.ofHom` triggers).
368	            (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (β.app (Opposite.op W.unop.left)).hom).map
369	                (φ.app (Opposite.op (Over.mk (Hom.opensFunctor f |>.map W.unop.hom)))) ≫
370	              -- leg-B: codomain unit ring-iso swap `restrictScalars β_W (𝟙_X(fW')) ⟶ 𝟙_Y(W')`,
371	              -- supplied by the named `dualUnitRingSwap` (= `inv (ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv))`,

377	      invFun := ?_
378	      map_add' := ?_
379	      map_smul' := ?_
380	      left_inv := ?_

587	noncomputable def dual_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
588	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M : X.Modules) :
589	    (dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f) := by
590	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`.
591	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M) ≪≫ ?_
592	  -- Step 2. Sheafification commutes with pullback.
593	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
594	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) ≪≫ ?_
595	  -- Step 3. Strip the outer sheafification, descending to the presheaf residual.
596	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
597	      (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
598	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL): the presheaf goal
599	  --   `(pullback φ).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((M.restrict f).val)`.
600	  -- H1: replace `pullback φ` with `pushforward β` (β the open-immersion structure ring iso).
601	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
602	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
603	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
604	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
605	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
606	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
607	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
608	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
609	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
610	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
611	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
612	  -- Residual: `(pushforward β).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)`.
613	  -- Assemble sectionwise from `sliceDualTransport`.  The `isoMk` naturality square is the
614	  -- thin-poset `Opens Y` coherence of the `sliceDualTransport` family; it becomes routine once
615	  -- `sliceDualTransport`'s body is concrete (its `.hom` is currently a `sorry`, so the square
616	  -- cannot be discharged yet — it is left as the assembly residual, per the planner bar).
617	  refine PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun V => sliceDualTransport f M V) ?_

628	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
629	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=

632	/-- **The dual of the structure sheaf is the structure sheaf.** `dual 𝒪_Y ≅ 𝒪_Y`.
633	The presheaf-level dual of the monoidal unit `𝟙_` is the unit (evaluation at `1`),
634	sheafified and identified with the (already-sheaf) unit by the sheafification counit.
635	Mirrors `tensorObj_unit_iso` with the presheaf left unitor replaced by
636	`presheafDualUnitIso`. The third leg of the `dual_isLocallyTrivial` chain. -/
637	noncomputable def dual_unit_iso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
638	    dual (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
639	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
640	      (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := Y)) ≪≫
641	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
642	      (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)

719	    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
720	    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
721	  app W :=
722	    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
723	    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
724	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
725	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle

727	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
728	      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
729	  naturality := by
730	    intro A B φ
731	    have hBA : (unop B).left ≤ (unop A).left := ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).le
732	    let κ : (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left ⟶ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left :=
733	      (Opens.map (U i).ι.base).map (homOfLE hBA)
734	    have himgA : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left) = (unop A).left := by
735	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
736	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop A).hom.le
737	    have himgB : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left) = (unop B).left := by
738	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
739	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop B).hom.le
740	    -- naturality of the underlying ab-presheaf morphism of `f i`

742	    -- the two thin-poset square edges agree (`Opens X` is a thin poset)

745	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
746	    have hsubN : ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB)

752	        = M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm)) ≫
753	          (M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.map κ.op := by
754	      rw [(M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]
755	      exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _

760	          N.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op := by
761	      rw [(N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubN]
762	      exact N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
